assistant_message.content:
{
 "finding_id": "openscap_021",
  "rule_id": "Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity",
  "category": "safe_to_remediate",
  "confidence": 0.96,
  "rationale": "This STIG item configures a session timeout via systemd-logind, which is a standard hardening measure that does not affect core authentication mechanisms or system boot. The change is reversible and poses minimal risk of lockout when applied with appropriate timeout values.",
  "risk_factors": [
    "potential user session interruption if timeout is set too low",
    "requires editing logind.conf and reloading the service"
  ],
  "safe_next_steps": [
    "Edit /etc/systemd/logind.conf and add or modify [Login] section with StopIdleSessionSec= value",
    "Apply changes with 'systemctl daemon-reload'",
    "Restart the logind service using 'systemctl restart systemd-logind.service' or reboot if preferred",
    "Verify the setting with 'loginctl show-session $ID -p IdleHint' or by testing session timeout"
  ],
  "requires_reboot": false,
  "touches_authn_authz": true,
  "touches_networking": false,
  "touches_filesystems": false
}
